tata.FY2023-24: AMBIGUOUS: FY2023 is consolidated while FY2024 is standalone

sections.about: This metric measures waste disposed through methods other than incineration and landfilling under BRSR Principle 6, Essential Indicator on disposal route. BRSR allows entities to specify other disposal operations if significant. A lower value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: All four companies reported zero other disposal operations in FY2025. Tata improved from 2,304 MT in FY2024 to zero in FY2025, while Jindal had already eliminated a very large FY2023 other-disposal stream of 766,298.3 MT by FY2024. The FY2025 convergence suggests residual disposal is now primarily classified under landfill or incineration rather than other disposal.
sections.tataPositioning: Tata ranked joint 1st in FY2025 with zero other disposal; all peers were tied, so the gap is 0%. Tata’s trajectory improved from rank 3 in FY2023 and rank 4 in FY2024 to rank 1 in FY2025.
sections.targets: No other-disposal-specific target was identified. Broader targets include SAIL’s 100% solid waste utilisation aspiration and JSL’s Zero-Waste-to-Landfill certification by 2030.
sections.comparabilityNotes: This category is residual and may be affected by company-level classification practices.
